phrase.text: The MARPOL <Convention> requires that every oil tanker of 150 tons <gross tonnage> and above carries on board a shipboard oil pollution <emergency> plan <approved by> the Flag State Administration.

New MARPOL Regulation requires that every ship of 150 tons gross tonnage and above <certified> to carry noxious liquid substances <in bulk> carries on board a shipboard marine pollution emergency plan for <noxious liquid> substances approved by the Administration.
One <combined> plan on board is more practical than two separate plans <in the event of> an emergency.
In this case the title of such a combined plan should be ‘Shipboard marine pollution emergency plan’ <in order to> distinguish it from the two other emergency plans <mentioned> above.
MARPOL regulations <prescribe> that the plans have to be <in accordance with> guidelines developed by the IMO.
It is <relevant> to note that shipboard oil pollution emergency plans which have already been approved by the Administration <need not> be modified.
The main <objectives> of these guidelines are to assist shipowners in preparing shipboard marine pollution emergency plans <in conformity with> the <cited> regulations.
The main goal of these guidelines is to assist governments in <enacting> domestic laws which <give force to> and <implement> the cited regulations.
<In the interest of> uniformity, governments are requested <to refer to> these guidelines when preparing <appropriate> national regulations.
